title: Starry Night
description: A swirling night sky over a village, painted during van Gogh's stay at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.

title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
description: Often referred to as the 'Mona Lisa of the North,' this painting depicts a girl wearing an exotic dress and a large pearl earring.

title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
description: A dramatic depiction of a towering wave threatening boats near the coast of Kanagawa, with Mount Fuji visible in the background.

title: The Persistence of Memory
description: Melting pocket watches in a dreamlike landscape, one of the most recognizable works of Surrealism.

title: The Birth of Venus
description: Venus, the goddess of love, emerges from the sea as a fully grown woman, arriving at the shore.

title: Nighthawks
description: Four figures sit in a brightly lit downtown diner late at night, viewed through a large glass window from the dark street outside.

title: Water Lilies
description: Part of Monet's famous series depicting his flower garden at Giverny, focusing on the reflections and colors of the water surface.

title: The Kiss
medium: Oil and gold leaf on canvas
description: A couple locked in an intimate embrace, wrapped in elaborate gold-patterned robes, set against a gold background.

title: Guernica
description: A powerful anti-war statement responding to the bombing of the Basque town of Guernica during the Spanish Civil War.

title: The Scream
medium: Oil, tempera, and pastel on cardboard
description: A figure stands on a bridge with an agonized expression against a turbulent orange sky.

title: Mona Lisa
description: The world's most famous painting depicts a seated woman with an enigmatic smile, believed to be Lisa Gherardini. Leonardo's mastery of sfumato gives the subject her mysterious, atmospheric quality.

title: Las Meninas
description: A complex and enigmatic composition showing the Infanta Margarita surrounded by her ladies-in-waiting, with Velázquez himself visible painting at a large canvas. The work plays with mirrors, perspective, and the nature of representation.

title: The Last Supper
description: Painted on the refectory wall of a Milan convent, this monumental mural captures the dramatic moment Christ announces that one of his apostles will betray him, capturing twelve distinct emotional reactions.

title: American Gothic
description: One of the most recognizable images in 20th-century American art, depicting a farmer and his daughter standing before a wooden house. Wood used his sister and dentist as models for the austere, stoic figures.

title: A Sunday on La Grande Jatte
description: Seurat's monumental pointillist masterpiece depicts Parisians relaxing on an island in the Seine. Composed of millions of tiny dots of color, it took two years to complete and founded the Neo-Impressionist movement.

title: Wanderer above the Sea of Fog
description: A lone figure stands on a rocky precipice, gazing out over a vast landscape shrouded in mist. The quintessential Romantic image of solitary contemplation in the face of sublime nature.

title: Impression, Sunrise
description: The painting that gave Impressionism its name. Monet's loose, spontaneous brushwork captures the shimmer of morning light over the port of Le Havre, with an orange sun reflected on the misty water.

title: The Arnolfini Portrait
description: A double portrait of an Italian merchant and his wife, renowned for its meticulous detail and symbolic richness. The convex mirror in the background reflects two additional figures, one of whom may be van Eyck himself.

title: The Hay Wain
description: A tranquil scene of rural English life on the River Stour, with a horse-drawn cart fording the water beside Willy Lott's cottage. Constable's naturalistic depiction of the Suffolk landscape was revolutionary for its time.

title: Le Déjeuner sur l'herbe
description: Manet's scandalous submission to the 1863 Salon des Refusés shocked audiences with its juxtaposition of a nude woman and two fully clothed men in a contemporary outdoor setting, breaking sharply with academic tradition.

title: The Night Watch
description: Rembrandt's monumental group portrait of an Amsterdam militia company captures Captain Frans Banninck Cocq leading his men into action. The dynamic composition and dramatic chiaroscuro broke radically with the static conventions of group portraiture.

title: The Creation of Adam
description: Part of the Sistine Chapel ceiling, this iconic fresco depicts God reaching out to give life to Adam, their fingers nearly touching. The image has become one of the most replicated religious paintings in history.

title: The Two Fridas
description: A double self-portrait painted shortly after Kahlo's divorce from Diego Rivera, showing two versions of herself with hearts exposed and connected by a single artery. The work reflects her emotional duality and personal anguish.

title: Liberty Leading the People
description: Commemorating the July Revolution of 1830, Delacroix depicts the allegorical figure of Liberty as a bare-breasted woman holding the tricolor flag, leading a diverse group of revolutionaries over the bodies of fallen soldiers.

title: The School of Athens
description: A grand fresco in the Vatican depicting the greatest philosophers, mathematicians, and scientists of antiquity gathered together. Plato and Aristotle stand at the center, surrounded by figures including Socrates, Pythagoras, and Euclid.

title: Bal du moulin de la Galette
description: A vibrant scene of Parisians dancing and socializing at a popular Montmartre dance garden on a Sunday afternoon. Renoir's masterful depiction of dappled sunlight filtering through the trees captures the joy of bourgeois leisure.

title: Three Musicians
description: A vivid example of Synthetic Cubism, the painting shows three masked figures—a Pierrot, a Harlequin, and a monk—rendered as flat, brightly colored geometric shapes. Picasso assembled the composition like a paper collage translated into paint.

title: Self-Portrait with Bandaged Ear
description: Painted shortly after the infamous incident in Arles, van Gogh portrays himself bundled against the cold, his ear wrapped in a white bandage. The Japanese woodblock print on the wall behind him reflects his lifelong admiration for Eastern art.

title: The Third of May 1808
description: A harrowing depiction of Spanish civilians being executed by Napoleonic soldiers during the Peninsular War. Goya's unflinching portrayal of war's brutality marked a turning point in the depiction of historical violence.

title: The Card Players
description: Part of a five-painting series, Cézanne portrays Provençal peasants absorbed in a quiet game of cards. His structured, geometric approach to form and his use of color to model space anticipated the developments of Cubism.

title: The Fighting Temeraire
description: Turner's elegiac vision of the famed warship being towed up the Thames to be scrapped, with a blazing sunset symbolizing the end of an era. The painting elegantly contrasts the ghostly sailing vessel with the dark steam tug pulling it forward.

title: Bedroom in Arles
description: Van Gogh painted his simple bedroom in the Yellow House in Arles using bold, flat colors and tilted perspective. He intended the work to express absolute restfulness, with the strong hues meant to soothe the eye and mind.

title: The Elephant Celebes
description: A bizarre mechanical elephant-like creature dominates a desolate landscape, accompanied by a headless mannequin. Ernst drew inspiration from a photograph of a Sudanese corn bin, transforming an ordinary object into an unsettling Surrealist vision.

title: The Lovers
description: René Magritte's *The Lovers* (1928) shows a couple kissing with their heads veiled in white cloth, evoking themes of isolation and obscured intimacy.

title: Street, Berlin
description: Kirchner depicts the frenetic energy of pre-war Berlin with sharp, angular figures crowded onto a city street. Two cocottes in elaborate hats dominate the foreground, embodying the alienation and moral tension of modern urban life.
